'''Nothing stays stagnant forever...'''
Dreyfus Scale: '''4/5'''
'''Frequency: 1/5 (Rare)'''
'''Phenomenon 20''' can manifest anywhere in the Backrooms, albeit incredibly rarely or under specific circumstances.
'''Hazard: 5/5 (Catastrophic)'''
'''Phenomenon 20''' can change the makeup of many levels, and potentially take many lives with them.
'''Mystery: 5/5 (Mysterious)'''
'''Phenomenon 20''' is one of the least documented yet dangerous phenomena throughout the entirety of the Backrooms.
Description
'''Phenomenon 20''', also known as “Conflicting Level Alterations”, is an unconfirmed anomalous phenomenon that has potentially affected many levels throughout the Backrooms. This phenomenon is currently unconfirmed and has no backing evidence; its only purpose was to attempt to explain sudden changes in the environment at [[Level 480]] and many similar levels. It states that the phenomenon drastically alters the level's characteristics, transforming it into a polar opposite of its former version. The phenomenon is unpredictable; it is unknown if it can repeat or undergo multiple iterations, as anything related to it has only occurred once, if it even exists. Top scientists are working hard to meticulously research '''Phenomenon 20''' to prove its existence and gather extra information on both the level and phenomenon. Usually, '''Phenomenon 20''' events are drawn out over a long period of time, and the process of transformation can take approximately three minutes to several decades. Due to their nature, '''Phenomenon 20''' events are hard to observe internally, making '''Level 480’s''' 26/11/21 event the only recorded and archived case of a '''Phenomenon 20''' event. From the logs recorded during the 26/11/21 event, we can make a few observations about the process of '''Phenomenon 20''', and events proceed as follows: *The sky on the affected level suddenly and without warning turns black, despite the time of day. All anomalous properties within the level will cease, and any lights within the level will turn off or become busted. *All entrances and exits out of the affected level are blocked off, aside from a very risky noclip. The environment within the level becomes unstable, usually starting out as a house or any other object being missing but increasing over time. In addition, medium-to-long-range data transmission becomes impossible for reasons that are currently unknown. *The environment starts to warp and change, with the members of Base Midwest describing it as “liquid land." The affected level will also become very unstable, with reality falling apart within these channels. This process usually takes approximately two hours to five years to complete before transitioning into the next stage. *At this point, communication between the affected level and other levels becomes impossible. The instability of the land begins to stop, and the level will slowly become more and more stable and explorable. This process has been known to take several years to complete. *The affected level will reopen. Usually, affected levels will keep some old exits and entrances but will usually gain new ones due to the new nature of the affected level.
'''Theories become facts when you look deeper...'''

Hey Aaron, sorry to bother you on your day off, but I may have discovered something relating to the supposed '''Level 287''' incident. I stumbled on a journal entry on '''[[Level 4]]''' that I believe has come from '''[[Level 287]]'''. It appears to detail one's escape out of the level during the event. The thing is, the page was labeled as being written on the 12th of December, which was a similar date as to what happened on Level 480 and the entry itself describes numerous similarities to Level 480's event. So, what I am saying is that '''Level 287''' may have some connection to '''Level 480''' in some way, and that also may indicate that Phenomenon 20 is real in some shape or form. Imma look into it, and I will shoot a message when I have proof.

Sure thing, I backed up the file into a digital photocopier and sent it to my computer. Here it is:
'''https//:www.spreadsheet1.back'''
Date 12/3/21 I don't know what happened, the last thing I remember before this whole situation is everyone running around in circles and panicking. I run along with the crowd, before I suddenly fell to the floor and everything went black. My head hurt so fucking bad I would have probably been shocked if there was no concussion. The level I was on was now desolate, with no soul in sight, and was slowly becoming more and more unstable by the moment. Most of the potential exits I found were either locked or not appearing. I looked out the windows and noticed that instead of the traditional hotel parking lot exterior, it was replaced by a vantablack abyss. All the levels courtyards were the same, with a pitch-black void at the bottom. Every object within the rooms seemed intact, however they appeared to blend in with the level's now blank and desolate nature, and all character and sentimentality those objects one held were gone. The worst part, by far, is what happened later, before I fortunately escaped. The level appeared to warp and become unstable, with parts appearing to glitch at a fast rate. A threatening atmosphere now proliferated the air, and all I was focused on was running in hopes that I didn't die. After I took a nap next to a courtyard, an urge told me to jump into the abyss. Granted, it was not a very strong urge, but it was enough to freak me out regardless, and I quickly got out of there. Soon enough, I began noticing shadows, more like shadowlike beings, on the level, whether directly in front of me or within a room. They would quickly scurry away or cover in the corner like cowards. Never knew what their problem was, all I know was that they just appeared on the level like some foreign parasite. Eventually, I just gave up and ran, hoping to find some sort of escape. By sheer luck, the hallways began to transition, and I ended up in the middle of [[Level 4]], miraculously next to the base of the [[Backrooms Red Cross]]. Found out I was 90 pounds underweight, go figure. While I was here, I took the time to write down my thoughts before leaving. The nature of the transformation of the level I was on still leaves me pondering, and I will continually ask the question: "What happened here?"





The page "Phenomenon 20" was just updated on the M.E.G. Database.

Phenomenon 20
Dreyfus Scale: '''4/5'''
'''Frequency: 1/5 (Rare)'''
'''Phenomenon 20''' can manifest anywhere in the Backrooms, albeit incredibly rarely or under specific circumstances.
'''Hazard: 5/5 (Catastrophic)'''
'''Phenomenon 20''' can change the makeup of many levels, and potentially take many lives with them.
'''Mystery: 5/5 (Mysterious)'''
'''Phenomenon 20''' is one of the least documented yet dangerous phenomena throughout the entirety of the Backrooms.
Description
{{Blurimage|file=Ballsdex.png|caption=An AI generated representation of a level being affected by Phenomenon 20}} '''Phenomenon 20''', also known as “Conflicting Level Alterations”, is an anomalous phenomenon that has potentially affected many levels throughout the Backrooms. The phenomenon itself is unpredictable and can hit at any time. Levels afflicted by '''Phenomenon 20''' will undergo events that cause them to become warped and corrupted, eventually transforming them into polar opposite versions of their former selves. Usually, '''Phenomenon 20''' events are drawn out over a long period of time, and the process of transformation can take approximately three minutes to several decades. Due to their nature, '''Phenomenon 20''' events are hard to observe internally, however with collected logs, addenda, and research, we can make a few observations about the process of '''Phenomenon 20''', and events proceed as follows: *The sky on the affected level suddenly and without warning turns black, despite the time of day. All anomalous properties within the level will cease, and any lights within the level will turn off or become busted. *All entrances and exits out of the affected level are blocked off, aside from a very risky noclip. The environment within the level becomes unstable, usually starting out as a house or any other object being missing but increasing over time. In addition, medium-to-long-range data transmission becomes impossible for reasons that are currently unknown. *The environment starts to warp and change, with the members of Base Midwest describing it as “liquid land." The affected level will also become very unstable, with reality falling apart within these channels. This process usually takes approximately two hours to five years to complete before transitioning into the next stage. *At this point, communication between the affected level and other levels becomes impossible. The instability of the land begins to stop, and the level will slowly become more and more stable and explorable. This process has been known to take several years to complete. *The affected level will reopen. Usually, affected levels will keep some old exits and entrances but will usually gain new ones due to the new nature of the affected level. Levels affected by '''Phenomenon 20''' have a few similar characteristics between each other, which includes oddball properties, a grayscale and bland appearance, etc. Currently, it is unknown what causes '''Phenomenon 20''' to take place, and why only certain levels are targeted, making it one of the most mysterious and destructive phenomena in the Backrooms. As of now, only two levels have been confirmed to undergo Phenomenon 20 events: '''[[Level 480]]''', and '''[[Level 287]]'''. '''Level 480''' forms the only case of '''Phenomenon 20''' actually being observed, due to the only M.E.G outpost on the level, Base Midwest, remaining until stage 3 of '''Phenomenon 20''' to document the mysterious circumstance. '''Level 287''' was discovered in its current state after '''Phenomenon 20''' plagued the level, however the event has not been observed, with the only documentation coming from journal excerpts, information from level explorations, among others.

Colin - ''"Aaron, you're going to want to see this."'' Aaron - "What is it?" Colin - ''"So, I think I have just found something big relating to both 480 and 287, as well as UL-2.'' Aaron - "Level -96? You mean the art gallery thing that vanished?" Colin - ''"Yeah, and on the database, it shows that there are entrances that connect the two levels, one being a two-way system, however you know what other level had a bunch of entrances to both levels?"'' Aaron - "Which one?" Colin - ''"Level -96."'' Aaron - "Wait, wasn't -96 discovered around a month before the event on Level 480?" Colin - ''"Yeah."'' Aaron - "But why is this so important, I mean, the Backrooms is like that a lot." Colin - ''"Because wanderers who have been on Level -96 before it vanished have reported paintings that look a lot like the environments of both 480 and 287, in an almost photorealistic manner."'' Aaron - "So, Level -96 may have shared a connection with 480 and 287 in some way?" Colin - ''"That's right."'' Aaron - "What could this all mean?" Colin - ''"It means there's something more going on than we realize."''
